questions.questionText:
Write down the mathematical name for each of the following.
(a) A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides.
(b) A triangle with all three sides equal.
workingSteps: (a) A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides is a trapezium.
(b) A triangle with all three sides equal is an equilateral triangle.
questions.explanation: Key geometrical vocabulary: trapezium has one pair of parallel sides; equilateral triangle has three equal sides and three 60° angles.

questions.questionText:
Write down the name of each type of angle.
(a) An angle of exactly 90°.
(b) An angle between 90° and 180°.
(c) An angle between 180° and 360°.
questions.explanation: Angle types: acute < 90°, right = 90°, obtuse between 90° and 180°, reflex between 180° and 360°.

questions.context: The diagram shows a rectangle ABCD where AB = 10 cm and BC = 6 cm. M is the midpoint of AB and N is the midpoint of CD.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Write down the mathematical name for the quadrilateral AMND.
workingSteps: AMND has right angles at each corner and AM = ND = 5 cm, MN = AD = 6 cm, so it is a rectangle.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Write down the number of lines of symmetry of rectangle ABCD.
workingSteps: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry: one horizontal and one vertical through the centre.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Find the order of rotational symmetry of rectangle ABCD.
workingSteps: A rectangle maps onto itself after 180° and 360° rotation, so the order is 2.

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Calculate the perimeter of the rectangle ABCD.

parts.label: e
parts.questionText: Calculate the area of the rectangle ABCD.

parts.label: f
parts.questionText: The diagonal AC is drawn. Write down the mathematical name for angle BAC.
workingSteps: Angle BAC is the angle at A inside the triangle ABC. Since BC < AB this is less than 45°, so it is acute.
